summary: Added a decompiled version of sysdolphin baselib texture-object animation update logic, TObjUpdateFunc, guarded under NON_MATCHING because the current C did not yet reproduce the original jump table. The change also renamed HSD_TObjTev and HSD_TObjTevDesc GXColor field constant to konst so the TEV constant-color animation cases could use tobj->tev->konst.{r,g,b,a}.
what_changed: Declared external labels lbl_804055B8 and lbl_804D5C90 in tobj.c, with lbl_804D5C90 documented as the tobj.c filename string used for assertion context.
Added static TObjUpdateFunc(void* obj, u32 type, FObjData* val) under #ifdef NON_MATCHING.

Implemented a switch over HSD_A_T_* texture animation tracks, including TIMG image table selection, TCLT TLUT selection, blend values, texture transform components, LOD bias, and TEV constant/tev0/tev1 color channels.
Grouped rotation, translation, and scale animation cases through a shared mtxdirty label that sets TEX_MTX_DIRTY after writing the updated transform component.
Converted normalized float animation values to color bytes with casts like (u8)(255.0 * val->fv) for KONST, TEV0, and TEV1 channels.
Renamed GXColor constant to GXColor konst in both HSD_TObjTev and HSD_TObjTevDesc.
smart_moves: Kept the newly decompiled function behind NON_MATCHING instead of presenting it as matched while the jump-table layout was still unresolved.
Preserved the original assertion behavior for missing imagetbl in the HSD_A_T_TIMG case, including filename/assertion-label references and source line 276.
Used a shared goto label for texture-matrix dirtiness, matching the natural code-shape where multiple transform-track cases converge on the same flag update.
Adjusted the TEV struct field name from constant to konst to align with the animation enum spelling HSD_A_T_KONST_* and avoid conflict/confusion with the C keyword-like term constant.
decomp_lessons: Large switch statements in baselib animation callbacks may remain non-matching specifically because of jump-table generation even when individual case behavior is understood.
When matching animation update functions, map each HSD_A_T_* enum directly to the corresponding HSD_TObj field write and preserve shared fallthrough/convergence points such as dirty-flag updates.
Texture transform animation tracks should mark TEX_MTX_DIRTY after modifying rotate, translate, or scale fields.
Header field names may need to be corrected to support decompiled callsites; here, the TEV constant color field needed the name konst in both runtime and descriptor structs.
Assertion strings and line numbers can be important matching evidence in baselib functions and should be represented explicitly when present.
naming_conventions: Use TObjUpdateFunc for the static texture-object animation update callback.
Use HSD_A_T_* enum names for texture animation track cases, including HSD_A_T_TIMG, HSD_A_T_TCLT, HSD_A_T_BLEND, HSD_A_T_ROTX, HSD_A_T_TRAU, HSD_A_T_LOD_BIAS, HSD_A_T_KONST_R, and HSD_A_T_TS_BLEND.
Name the TEV constant color field konst, not constant, in HSD_TObjTev and HSD_TObjTevDesc.
Use TEX_MTX_DIRTY for the texture matrix dirty flag set after transform animation updates.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: The PR body and inline comment identify the remaining blocker as jump-table matching for the switch in TObjUpdateFunc.
The implementation uses #ifdef NON_MATCHING to document intended behavior without claiming binary equivalence.
A shared mtxdirty label is used to make multiple switch cases converge before setting tobj->flags |= TEX_MTX_DIRTY, a tactic often relevant to reproducing compiler control flow.
The explicit external labels lbl_804055B8 and lbl_804D5C90 preserve references needed by __assert in the TIMG case.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that remove NON_MATCHING from TObjUpdateFunc or resolve its jump table.
Search for additional uses of HSD_TObjTev.konst to confirm the field rename propagated consistently.
Compare other baselib animation update callbacks for shared dirty-label switch patterns.
